Article Title: Did Brock Lesnar retire? Here’s what his WrestleMania 42 gesture means
Article Text:
Brock Lesnar. Image via: Rich Freeda/WWE via Getty Images

Brock Lesnar hints at WWE retirement

Brock Lesnar hints at retirement. Image via: WWE

Oba Femi defeated Brock Lesnar in WrestleMania 42



Brock Lesnar's WWE legacy



Watch Brock Lesnar's WWE Debut Brock Lesnar's WWE Debut

Brock Lesnar will forever be counted among the greats, as he had a stellar resume and an astonishing career in the promotion. Of course, nothing lasts forever, and things go on. After his loss to Oba Femi, it appears that he is all set to say goodbye to his wrestling career.Lesnar showed his rare side; he appeared to be in tears and with a heavy heart. Certainly, it was a rare sight for any WWE fan. Fans inside Allegiant Stadium watched closely as one of WWE’s most dominant figures stood still, almost reflective, before making a gesture that instantly shifted the mood in the arena.It didn’t come with a speech or a formal announcement. Instead, it came through silence, body language, and a series of actions that longtime viewers recognize all too well.The moment arrived right after his defeat to Oba Femi. Brock Lesnar sat up, stared ahead, then slowly removed his gloves. A pause followed. Then the boots came off. He placed both in the center of the ring, kissed the mat, and embraced Paul Heyman. The crowd responded with a mix of shock and appreciation as Lesnar waved before walking away.Ariel Helwani captured the emotion of the scene: “An emotional Brock Lesnar has removed his gloves and shoes after losing to Oba Femi at Mania.He put them in the middle of the ring and waved to the crowd. And then a big hug with his long-time friend, Paul Heyman. Is this really it? Sure seemed that way.”Even within the industry, the reaction was immediate. Kurt Angle wrote, “Love you my brother. There will only be one like you. Enjoy retirement Brock.” Mark Kaboly added, “Brock Lesnar sure doesn't seem to be a guy who will come back for one more match. This one will be talked about for ages. #wrestlemania”Still, WWE has not confirmed anything officially.The match itself told a surprising story. Oba Femi controlled long stretches and absorbed Lesnar’s signature offense, including the F5. That alone raised eyebrows. The finish came when Femi delivered a chokeslam followed by a powerful Fall From Grace to secure a decisive pin.Reports suggest Lesnar personally chose Femi as his opponent months ago, which adds another layer to the result. If this was meant to pass the torch, it did so in a dramatic way. Still, the one-sided nature of the finish left some questioning whether a legend like Lesnar deserved a more competitive farewell.Michael Cole hinted during commentary that this “could probably be goodbye,” though he stopped short of confirming anything.Lesnar debuted in 2002 and quickly became the youngest WWE Champion of his time. Across multiple runs, he captured world titles, won Royal Rumbles, and headlined WrestleMania on five occasions.His career stretched beyond WWE as well. A run in the UFC brought him the heavyweight title, adding to his already rare résumé. In 2014, he delivered one of wrestling’s most shocking moments by ending The Undertaker’s undefeated WrestleMania streak.Yet, away from the spotlight, Lesnar often kept things simple. “For me, none of my championships or none of my accolades, they all mean nothing, boys,” he once said. “My kids are my legacy.”That perspective now feels more relevant than ever as questions swirl about his future.
Article Summary: Brock lesnar lost to oba femi at wrestlemania 42. Lesnar appeared to be in tears and with a heavy heart. He removed his gloves and shoes and embraced paul heyman. Wwe has not confirmed anything officially about lesnar's future.

Article Title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: 'Jitni der crease par hoon, match chheen sakta hoon': Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's bold warning to rivals - WATCH
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (Image credit: BCCI/IPL)

Watch Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling. Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling.

NEW DELHI: See the ball and launch it over the ropes - that’s the mantra young batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi follows whenever he is at the crease. After setting the Under-19 World Cup on fire, where he scored a blazing 175 in the final to power India to the title win earlier this year, Sooryavanshi is now carrying on his fiery form in the IPL 2026.In IPL 2026, the 15-year-old from Samastipur, Bihar, is doing what he does best - sending bowlers on a leather hunt with his power-hitting.Sooryavanshi is Rajasthan Royals ’ highest run-getter in IPL 2026 so far, scoring 246 runs in 6 matches at an average of 41.00 and a breathtaking strike rate of 236.54.In fact, Sooryavanshi himself confessed in an interview that if he stays at the crease for long, he can take the game away from the opposition."Main jitni der bhi ground pe hoon, utni der mein dominate karke agar khelu, to match unke haath se chheen sakta hoon [For whatever duration I am on the ground, as long as I am using my skills, dominate and play, I can snatch the match from opponents’ hands]," Sooryavanshi said on Doordarshan."Maine apne father se bhi baat ki - hamesha bola ki mera aadha dream poora ho gaya hai, aadha abhi baaki hai. Senior level par World Cup khelna har player ka ek hi dream hota hai - India ke liye khelna aur jeetna.Abhi mera poora focus isi par hai ki senior India ke liye jitna ho sake utna contribute karun [I also spoke to my father. He said I have fulfilled half of his dream, but half is still left, I need to play in the World Cup for the senior Indian team. Of course, every player shares the same dream: to play for the seniors and win for their country, India. That is my focus for India]," he said.In 13 IPL matches so far in his career, he has scored 498 runs at an average of 38.30, with a strike rate of 220.35, including a century and three fifties. He has smashed 42 fours and 44 sixes off 226 balls.Sooryavanshi also drew praise from former India cricketer Irfan Pathan, who called him a "special player" with a bright future ahead.He added that the youngster has the potential to stay at the top level for many years if he continues developing at this pace and temperament."The theory of Vaibhav Suryavanshi needing big-name bowlers in the opposition is proving true again ;) This kid is a special player with many years at the top level ahead of him," Pathan wrote in an X post.
Article Summary: Sooryavanshi is rajasthan royals’ highest run-getter in ipl 2026 so far. He has scored 246 runs in 6 matches at an average of 41.00 and a strike rate of 236.54. Former india cricketer irfan pathan called him a "special player" with a bright future.

Article Title: Afghanistan's Rashid Khan was offered Indian citizenship by 'high-ranking official'; here's how he responded
Article Text:
Rashid Khan

Rashid Khan: From Streets to Stardom

Watch Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling. Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling.

Rashid Khan was approached by two countries, including India, with offers of citizenship and a chance to represent them, but he turned them down and chose to continue playing for Afghanistan, according to a new book.In, set to be released on Monday, Rashid told author Mohammad Haand Jafar that both India and Australia made such offers."I received such offers (citizenship and playing) from both Australia and India. But I told them, ‘If I don’t play for my country, I won’t play for any other country either," Khan is quoted as saying in the book, news agency PTI reported.While he did not go into details about the Australian offer, the 27-year-old described the Indian approach during the 2023 IPL season.Rashid, who plays in the IPL for Gujarat Titans, said a team official told him that a “high-ranking official from the Indian Cricket Board” wanted to meet him."I went over and greeted him. We started talking, and he said: ‘The situation in your country (Afghanistan) is very bad. Come stay in India. We will give you Indian documents, live here, play cricket here.’ I was surprised by what he was saying and didn't know how to respond.But I smiled and said, ‘Thank you very much. I am playing for my country, Afghanistan,'" Khan said.Rashid’s stance on representing Afghanistan has come up earlier as well.As mentioned in the book, during the 2018 IPL, while playing for Sunrisers Hyderabad against Kolkata Knight Riders, he delivered an all-round performance, scoring 34 off 10 balls and taking three wickets.The performance led to social media posts in India asking the government to grant him citizenship, with users tagging then External Affairs Minister Sushma Swaraj.Responding to those posts, Swaraj tweeted: “I saw all your tweets. But citizenship matters are dealt with by the Ministry of Home Affairs.”The issue also drew a reaction from former Afghan President Ashraf Ghani, who said Rashid was a matter of national pride and would not be given to another country.Rashid later thanked him and said he would continue to represent Afghanistan.The book follows Rashid’s journey from Nangarhar to international cricket. It covers how he missed out on an Afghanistan U-19 trial before becoming one of the country’s leading players and later being named the ICC’s Player of the Decade in 2020.
Article Summary: Rashid khan says he was offered citizenship and a chance to play for india and australia. But he chose to continue playing for afghanistan, according to a new book. The 27-year-old was named the icc’s player of the decade in 2020.

Article Title: Brock Lesnar: WWE WrestleMania 42 Night 2 Results & Highlights: Roman Reigns wins World title after 2 years, Brock Lesnar signals retirement, and more
Article Text:
Image via WWE

Brock Lesnar vs. Oba Femi

Six-Pack Challenge Ladder Match



Sami Zayn vs. Trick Williams

"The Demon" Finn Balor vs. Dominik Mysterio



Jade Cargill vs. Rhea Ripley

Roman Reigns vs. CM Punk

WrestleMania 42 was back in the Allegiant Stadium for the second night of The Show of Shows. The event kicked off with John Cena once again welcoming the audience, and then a huge collision between two dominant forces. Following that, the show saw some classic in-ring showdowns and moments. Finally, WrestleMania Sunday was capped off with the main event showdown that saw Roman Reigns defeat CM Punk and become the World Champion after two years.This was the opening match for WrestleMania Sunday. Brock Lesnar and Oba Femi clashed in a major match. Despite being a newcomer, Femi showed fierceness against The Beast and did not back down. Following a hellicious in-ring encounter, the ending saw Femi counter the F-5 and take down Lesnar with the Fall From Grace to win the match. After that, Lesnar left his boots and gloves in the ring and shared an emotional moment with Paul Heyman, hinting at retirement.Penta defended his WWE Intercontinental title against Rey Mysterio, JD McDonagh, Rusev, Je'Von Evans, and Dragon Lee. It was a highly classic ladder match that saw jaw-dropping spots, feats, and moves executed by all superstars.In the final moments, Penta hit the Destroyer on Evans and climbed the ladder to grab his title.Sami Zayn defended his WWE United States Championship against Trick Williams, with Lil Hatchy. The new rising star and the Underdog from the Underground delivered an exceptional match. It came to a close after Williams countered Zayn in the corner and hit the Trick Shot for the win.Finn Balor brought back his alter-ego, The Demon King, to battle Dominik Mysterio in a Street Fight. Balor clearly handed the upper hand throughout the match and ended with a victory after delivering a Coup de Grâce through the table on Mysterio.Rhea Ripley challenged Jade Cargill for the WWE Women's Championship. The match saw interference from Cargill's allies, B-Fab and Michin, but was neutralized by Ripley's ally, IYO Sky. In the end, Ripley snuck out of a Jaded attempt and planted Cargill with the Riptide to win the match and become the new champion.The main event of the night saw CM Punk defend his World Heavyweight Championship against Roman Reigns. It was one of the most captivating main events in WrestleMania history. Both men inflicted major damage on each other as the World title hung in the balance. The final moments of the showdown saw Reigns delivering multiple spears on Punk and scoring the pinfall to become the new World Champion.Also read: Did Brock Lesnar retire? Here’s what his WrestleMania 42 gesture means
Article Summary: Wrestlemania 42 was back in the allegiant stadium for the second night of the show of shows. Brock lesnar and oba femi clashed in a major match. Sami zayn defended his wwe united states championship against trick williams. Finn balor brought back his alter-ego to battle dominik mysterio in a street fight.

Article Title: Kl Rahul: KL Rahul's long-standing record shattered! Priyansh Arya creates history in style
Article Text:
Priyansh Arya and KL Rahul (Image credit: BCCI/IPL)

Watch Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling. Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling.

Priyansh Arya

KL Rahul

NEW DELHI: Star young batter Priyansh Arya seems to love clearing the ropes - and he does it against any bowling attack. During the IPL 2026 clash against Lucknow Super Giants, Arya unleashed brutal hitting, smashing 93 off just 37 balls to power Punjab Kings to a mammoth 254/7. He was well supported by fellow left-hander Cooper Connolly, who struck 87 off 46 deliveries.At a packed Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, PBKS defended their season-high total of 254/7 by restricting LSG to 200/5. With this win, the in-form Punjab Kings extended their strong run to stay at the top of the points table, while LSG slumped to their third straight defeat and dropped to eighth place.Arya, who hammered four boundaries and nine sixes, tore into the LSG bowlers from the outset and raced to his fifty in just 19 deliveries with relentless strokeplay.In the process, he went past KL Rahul for the most fifties scored in under 20 balls in the franchise’s history. Arya’s 93 came at a stunning strike rate of 251.35, and he looked set for a well-deserved century before falling seven runs short - caught at long-off by Mitchell Marsh off Manimaran Siddharth.Arya was adjudged Player of the Match for his explosive knock.He now has three fifties in under 20 balls - including a 19-ball half-century against LSG this season, a 16-ball effort against Sunrisers Hyderabad, and another 19-ball fifty against Chennai Super Kings last season.Rahul, in comparison, had recorded a 14-ball half-century against Delhi Capitals in 2018 and a 19-ball fifty against Chennai Super Kings in 2019.In five innings for PBKS this season, Arya has scored 211 runs at an average of 42.20 and a strike rate of 248.23, including two fifties. He has smashed 14 fours and 20 sixes so far.Overall, in 22 matches for Punjab Kings, Arya has accumulated 686 runs at an average of 31.18 and a strike rate of 196.00, with a century and four fifties.Batters with multiple 50+ scores in under 20 balls for Punjab Kings in IPL history:- 3(vs LSG Today | vs SRH 2026 | vs CSK 2025)- 2(vs CSK 2019 | vs DD 2018)
Article Summary: Priyansh arya smashed 93 off just 37 balls to power punjab kings to 254/7. Arya went past kl rahul for the most fifties scored in under 20 balls in the franchise’s history. He was adjudged player of the match for his explosive knock.

Article Title: 'My bat is the prize': Shreyas Iyer reveals dressing-room contest behind Punjab Kings run-fest
Article Text:
Image credit: BCCI/IPL

Watch Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling. Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling.

NEW DELHI: Punjab Kings captain Shreyas Iyer was all smiles when he came for the post-match presentation after his side registered a big 54-run win over Lucknow Super Giants in Mullanpur on Sunday. With this win, Punjab Kings jumped to the top of the table with 5 wins in 6 matches, 11 points, and a net run rate of +1.420.After the massive victory, Iyer revealed that a light-hearted six-hitting contest inside the dressing room has been the reason behind Punjab Kings' batting blitz.Devastating batters Priyansh Arya and Cooper Connolly made a mockery of the Lucknow Super Giants bowlers and powered their team to a mammoth 254/7 in 20 overs.Arya and Connolly smashed 93 and 87 respectively as PBKS posted 254/7 - the highest total of this IPL season. The duo added 182 runs in just 13.2 overs for a hugely entertaining second-wicket stand, taking PBKS past Royal Challengers Bengaluru’s 250/3 - the previous highest total this season.In reply, LSG managed 200/5 and suffered a 54-run defeat."Exceptional partnership, some shots were jaw-dropping. Hitting fast bowlers off the back-foot straight.Composure in the middle overs was sensational," Iyer said during the post-match presentation."Was talking with Cooper and Arya, said let's compete for the most no. of sixes. Prize is my bat, doesn't make sense but hope it'll make us happy. When you let players be, I think they just deliver. That's our mantra.""I don't tell them how to go about their specific innings, they have their patterns set. Ricky Ponting motivates just before the game, I chip in, boys come in and deliver."Punjab Kings will next face Delhi Capitals on April 25.
Article Summary: Shreyas iyer's punjab kings beat lucknow super giants by 54 runs. Priyansh arya and cooper connolly hit 93 and 87 respectively. Pbks posted 254/7 - the highest total of this ipl season. Iyer reveals light-hearted six-hitting contest inside dressing room.

Article Title: Ashok Sharma: India's 150kmph bowling hope still lives in village without roads - Inside Ashok Sharma's dream
Article Text:
Ashok Sharma of Gujarat Titans celebrates after taking a wicket (AP)

Unki Thar nikal jaati hai, aapki bhi nikal jayegi

Watch Kagiso Rabada press conference: On Shubman Gill, GT win and net run-rate Kagiso Rabada press conference: On Shubman Gill, GT win and net run-rate

Sharam aati hai logon ko bulaane mein. Raasta hi nahi hai

Ashok Sharma's father Nathulal Sharma (left) and his elder brother Akshay Sharma (Photo Credit: Pratyush Raj)

Sukoon hai idhar

Kuch bhi ho jaaye, apna gaon nahi chhodenge

Ashok loves it here. He wakes up at 5, goes to the ground for running, trains for three hours, comes back at 8 and then sleeps again. He has become very conscious of his diet, especially after the knee injury he suffered during his stint with KKR. Akshay Sharma

Gehun ki katai chal rahi hai

Ashok Sharma's father Nathulal Sharma with his electric scooter gifted by his son. (Photo Courtesy: Pratyush Raj)

chaanch

ye Akshay aur Ashok ko bhi kaafi pasand hai, ghanton cricket khelne ke baad

I used to enjoy intimidating batters. It gave me a sense of satisfaction, and when they got scared, it felt like I had achieved something. Akshay Sharma

Akshay with his daughter Raja in her lap, while his father Nathulal Sharma watches on (Photo Courtesy: Pratyush Raj)

Ashok was always breaking things. He broke so many items. Because of him, Akshay used to get beaten up. Lali Devi

Nahi

Ashok Sharma (left) with his elder brother Akshay Sharma (Special Arrangement)

Agar Akshay shaant hai toh Ashok shaitaan

Tod phod poore din bas. Kitna samaan toda hai usne. Uske chakkar mein Akshay ki pitai ho jaati thi

Ek baar toh papa ne belt se bhi peeta tha iske chakkar mein

Ashok Sharma's mother Lali Devi (Photo Courtesy: Pratyush Raj)

Ashok Sharma's dream car Thar outside his home in Rampura.

In 2023, when he was with KKR, towards the end of the season I asked him what he wanted for his birthday. He immediately said, I want a Thar. If you do not buy it, I will not come home. Akshay Sharma

Ashok Sharma with his late coach Vivek Yadav (Ashok Sharma | Instagram)

Rampura (Jaipur): "(His car gets through, yours will too)," a young boy quips when asked about the location of India's new pace sensation Ashok Sharma's house.Ashok Sharma may be making a lot of noise with his pace in the ongoing Indian Premier League (IPL), but reaching his village, around 35 km from Rajasthan's capital Jaipur, is an uphill task. There is a sudden exit on the Delhi-Mumbai Expressway and a few kilometres down that dusty lane, after carefully navigating the hastily operated railway crossings, the road disappears. That is when you understand the true meaning of "tighten your seatbelts" and the divide between the two Indias.(I feel embarrassed to invite people over. "There isn’t even a proper road," says Akshay Sharma, Ashok's elder brother, while welcoming us to his home. The location pin ends a few hundred metres before the residence, requiring crafty maneuvering on the narrow lanes with several hairpin turns.Ashok's village reflects a true sense of rural India. There are double-storey houses, coolers remain a prized asset, and proper roads have not yet reached the village.Yet, there is a sense of peace, which Akshay says is the reason the family has chosen not to leave their roots.(It is peaceful here). Ashok also loves it. Everything started from here, whether it was me playing cricket or him taking big strides. Ashok always says, '' (Whatever happens, we will never leave our ancestral village)," says Akshay, while dusting off the charpai under one of the two neem trees in front of their house. The front yard still remains a comfortable space in the Sharma household, offering ample space to house two 'charpaais' and host guests in shade, with the birds offering a soothing background score.It is rare to see a present-day Indian cricketer, who has already played Ranji Trophy and the IPL, still living in his village. The amount of money Indian cricketers earn, especially with the advent of the IPL, is massive, and a luxurious lifestyle often follows. However, Ashok Sharma believes in simplicity and focuses entirely on his performance. Akshay says his brother is not concerned about such luxuries."He loves it here. He wakes up at 5, goes to the ground for running, trains for three hours, comes back at 8 and then sleeps again. He has become very conscious of his diet, especially after the knee injury he suffered during his stint with KKR. He always says that he would exchange the lavish five-star life for this any day. His diet is also simple. He loves homemade food. Diet, recovery, and sleep are the three most important parts of his training routine now," he adds.Early in the chat, Ashok and Akshay's father, Nathulal Sharma, joins the conversation from his electric scooter, a gift from his younger son. Wearing a torn shirt and mud-stained trousers, he had just returned from a shift in the fields and caught his breath before settling into the charpai. "(The wheat harvest is underway)," he says with a smile.In the meantime, Lali Devi, Ashok's mother, brings a glass offor everyone. The traditional steel glass, the drink's thick texture, and the right amount of home spices made it refreshing at the first sip. Lali Devi had a smile on her face when the drink was gulped within seconds and she offered a refill before adding, "" (This is something Akshay and Ashok also really like, especially after playing cricket for hours).Ashok has always looked up to Akshay as his inspiration. Akshay sowed the seed of fast bowling in his younger brother, starting with the satisfaction they received in instilling fear in the batters heads. What started as fun in the nearby fields soon became an obsession to set the speed gun on fire."We both were quick. Batters ko daraane mein maza aata tha, ek tasalli milti thi, aur jab wo darte the toh lagta tha ki kuch kiya hai (I used to enjoy intimidating batters. It gave me a sense of satisfaction, and when they got scared, it felt like I had achieved something)," recalls Akshay.Fast bowling came naturally to the Sharma brothers. Growing up, they had heard stories of their uncle, Ram Dayal Sharma, a fearsome fast bowler in the village. He still lives in the opposite house and would be proud to see his nephew carry on the intimidating tradition."I have not even seen him bowl. He quit cricket long ago, but we have heard he was very quick. He used to get special invitations to play in nearby villages. He had long hair, just like MS Dhoni," he says.Nathulal Sharma, sitting nearby and keeping an eye on his two-year-old granddaughter Raja, lit up at the mention. "My brother was quick. These two have followed in his footsteps," he adds.For the Sharma family, Ashok is living the unfulfilled dreams of his uncle and brother. On March 30, the family learned that Ashok, who had previously been with Kolkata Knight Riders and Rajasthan Royals, would make his IPL debut for Gujarat Titans.Excited, the family drove to Chandigarh, around 450 km from their village, and reached just in time to watch Ashok play live for the first time."We have been to the stadium before as well. He had given us passes when he was with Rajasthan Royals, but this felt different," says Akshay.A day later, Ashok invited his family to Ahmedabad for Gujarat's second match. Instead of taking a flight, they chose to travel by train, a journey of nearly 10 hours from Jaipur.When asked if he had ever travelled by flight, Nathulal Sharma gently nods and says, "(not yet)." Akshay jokingly adds, "(He is scared of flying)." Lali Devi, who has been listening quietly, joins in and says, "(We will travel by flight someday)."For the village, Ashok has become a superstar. Anyone you ask proudly directs you to his house, because he is one of their own. But for Nathulal and Lali Devi, he remains the mischievous child, and everyone has a an almost similar story.Nathulal Sharma says, "(If Akshay is calm, then Ashok is mischievous)."Akshay quickly adds, "He used to bunk school. He always came up with some strange excuse to leave early."Lali Devi further adds, "(He was always breaking things. He broke so many items. Because of him, Akshay used to get beaten up)."Akshay smiles and adds, "(Once, Papa even beat me with a belt because of him)."Despite all his mischief, Ashok, the youngest sibling, remains the family's favourite child."In 2023, when he was with KKR, towards the end of the season I asked him what he wanted for his birthday. He immediately said, 'Thar chahiye. Nahi doge toh ghar nahi aaunga' (I want a Thar. If you do not buy it, I will not come home)," laughs Akshay.Ashok's dream car now stands covered outside their house and is a major attraction for the children in the village. Considering the state of the roads in the village, an off-road capable vehicle was a smart choice.The family remains grateful to Ashok's mentor Vivek Yadav, a former Ranji Trophy cricketer, who took him under his wing and was immediately impressed by his pace."In 2019, when he was 17, I took him to the Aravali Cricket Academy, where Vivek bhaiya was impressed by his pace. Ashok was erratic, but he directly placed him in the senior team. He asked me to let Ashok stay at the academy, and it helped him become a better cricketer," says Akshay.However, the Covid pandemic changed everything. The lockdown forced Ashok to return home, where the road in front of his house became his training ground. The biggest blow came on May 5, 2021, when Vivek Yadav, a two-time Ranji Trophy champion with Rajasthan, passed away due to complications."He did not speak for weeks. We thought the dream was over. No one knew us and we were clueless. But Vikas bhaiya called and told us that he would fulfil his brother's dream, and the academy would do everything to help him reach his potential," recalls Akshay.After being picked by Gujarat Titans for Rs 90 lakh last year, Ashok had shared an emotional message about his mentor."It was the last time I cried. I thought my career was over. He wanted me to play for India. He used to say, 'You will play for India very soon, you will play top-level cricket very soon'," Ashok had said.It is going to be a long road for Ashok to achieve that dream. But the 23-year-old fast bowler, definitely knows the importance of patience and perseverance, and that he is just off the blocks.At the Aravali Cricket Academy, coaches Vikas Yadav, Jagminder Singh, and others remain hopeful that Ashok will fulfil Vivek Yadav's dream. Back home, Akshay, who gave up cricket for his brother, hopes that with Ashok's success, a proper road will finally reach Rampura, a village which might end India's prolonged wait for an out-and-out fast bowler.
Article Summary: Ashok sharma's village is 35 km from rajasthan's capital jaipur. He wakes up at 5, goes to the ground for running, trains for three hours, comes back at 8 and then sleeps again. He has become very conscious of his diet, especially after the knee injury he suffered during his stint with kkr.

Article Title: Aryna Sabalenka fiancé Georgios Frangulis addresses critics questioning his presence during her tennis matches
Article Text:
Aryna Sabalenka and Georgios Frangulis (Image Via Getty)

Money Buys Happiness podcast



Aryna Sabalenka and Georgios Frangulis relationship and response to criticism explained

Esquire

There has been a lot of talk around Aryna Sabalenka and her fiancé Georgios Frangulis. Many fans have noticed him sitting in the stands during her matches, and not everyone has been supportive. Some people even questioned how he manages his business while attending so many games. Now, Georgios has finally responded, and his message was very direct.In a video shared by theon Instagram, Georgios made it clear that he is not bothered by the criticism. He spoke openly about his relationship and how they support each other. At the same time, he also pushed back at people who keep judging his choices and his presence during Sabalenka’s matches.Aryna Sabalenka and Georgios Frangulis started dating in 2024, and earlier this year, they announced their engagement. Since then, Georgios has often been seen in her player box during big matches. While many fans enjoy seeing that support, others have raised questions about his work and time.Georgios addressed this directly in his statement. He said, “For me to have someone that I love that trusts me and looks up to me.I can teach and learn from, with my fiancé. With Aryna it’s all upside. I don’t see any downside.”He then spoke about the criticism he receives and how he handles his work. He explained that watching matches does not stop him from managing his responsibilities. He said, “Believe me I get criticism. How can he run the company. He’s always watching tennis. You see me on TV and I am watching tennis. It’s a two-hour match. Like I was doing a meeting before I’m gonna do another after. Not your business what I am doing.”Georgios also praised Sabalenka’s success and their shared understanding. He added, “She’s super accomplished. We can enjoy each other’s success, help each other’s failures.”On the other side, Sabalenka has also spoken about how important this relationship is for her. In an interview with, she shared a simple lesson she has learned. She said, “You see that each area of life takes the same dedication, discipline, and hard work.”While all this is happening off the court, Sabalenka’s form on the court has been strong. She started the season by winning the Brisbane International and then reached the final of the Australian Open. She later completed the Sunshine Double by winning both Indian Wells and the Miami Open, beating top players like Elena Rybakina and Coco Gauff.She was set to play at the Stuttgart Open but pulled out due to an injury picked up in Miami. Even so, both her performance and her personal life continue to stay in the spotlight.
Article Summary: Aryna sabalenka and georgios frangulis started dating in 2024. Georgios has often been seen in her player box during big matches. While many fans enjoy seeing that support, others have raised questions about his work and time.

Article Title: Connor McDavid’s wife Lauren Kyle celebrates emotional milestone as Oilers captain gears up for electrifying Stanley Cup playoff run
Article Text:
Connor McDavid’s wife (Getty Images)

Connor McDavid’s wife reacts to milestone ahead of Oilers’ high-stakes playoff campaign

The Edmonton Oilers closed their regular season with purpose, delivering a 6-1 win over the Vancouver Canucks that did more than pad the standings. It set a tone. At the center of it, Connor McDavid dictated play with four assists, wrapping up another elite campaign. The result secured second place in the Pacific Division and home-ice advantage for the opening round. Off the ice, the moment carried a personal note as Lauren Kyle shared her pride publicly.Connor McDavid’s final outing wasn’t just productive. It felt controlled, almost routine for a player operating at this level. His four assists helped power an early surge, including a standout opening period where Matt Savoie recorded his first NHL hat trick. The Oilers never looked back from there.After the game, McDavid didn’t overstate it, but he made it clear the win mattered beyond the scoreboard. “It was an important one. We wanted to start here at home and give ourselves the best chance in the first round. The start was great,” he said.That clarity extended to his line’s development. There has been a noticeable shift in chemistry, especially with Savoie and Zach Hyman alongside him. “(Me and Savoie are) building something a little bit, playing together for a little while now.We’re starting to understand each other a little bit. (Zach Hyman) came in and did exactly what he does, forechecks hard and fills that net-front spot, which frees up a lot of space for us. ”Numbers back up the impact. McDavid secured the Art Ross Trophy with 138 points, his sixth time leading the league. That places him alongside Mario Lemieux and Gordie Howe, trailing only Wayne Gretzky in all-time scoring titles. It’s rare company, and it adds weight to what already feels like a defining stretch in his career.Away from the numbers, Lauren Kyle’s posts from Rogers Place gave a glimpse into the moment. A shot of the scoreboard came with a simple line, “Off to the playoffs!!” Another message, just as direct, read, “So proud of you.” It wasn’t elaborate, but it didn’t need to be. The context spoke for itself.Edmonton’s wider performance also offered encouragement. Ryan Nugent-Hopkins contributed offensively, while Colton Dach added a Gordie Howe hat trick. Cleaner puck movement and sharper finishing stood out, areas that had wavered at times earlier in the schedule.The focus now shifts quickly. A first-round matchup with the Anaheim Ducks begins April 20. McDavid’s form is a given at this point. The question is whether the rhythm building around him holds under playoff pressure.
Article Summary: Edmonton oilers beat vancouver canucks 6-1 to clinch second place in the pacific division. Connor mcdavid had four assists in the win, his sixth art ross trophy. His wife, lauren kyle, posted a message to her husband from rogers place.

Article Title: India's next T20I captain? 'Self-aware' Shreyas Iyer breathing down Suryakumar's neck
Article Text:
Shreyas Iyer (Image credit: AFP Photo)

Watch Ajinkya Rahane press conference: On another defeat, Cameron Green and more Ajinkya Rahane press conference: On another defeat, Cameron Green and more

Watch Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling. Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling.

Poll Who do you think has been the more impactful captain in IPL history? Gautam Gambhir Ricky Ponting Shreyas Iyer

NEW CHANDIGARH: Shreyas Iyer first tasted IPL captaincy in turbulent circumstances. In an awkward press conference midway through the 2018 season, flanked by head coach Ricky Ponting , Delhi Daredevils (now Delhi Capitals) stalwart Gautam Gambhir stood down owing to woeful form and the team’s embarrassing run. Gambhir announced Iyer as his successor.Iyer went on to blossom as a captain and a batter under Ponting at Capitals before moving to Kolkata Knight Riders in 2022, when the Delhi franchise shifted leadership to Rishabh Pant. He reunited with Gambhir at KKR in 2024 and guided the franchise to the IPL title. The triumph saw Gambhir graduate to India’s head coach within a month, and Iyer depart the franchise.Even as Gambhir went into overdrive to reshape India’s T20I setup, Iyer remained out of contention. Turbulence has followed his career despite sustained consistency. Now reunited with Ponting at Punjab Kings — both nursing scars from their Capitals exit — Iyer is stirring a campaign that could knock on the door of Gambhir’s champion T20 unit.On Sunday evening here, Iyer will come up against Pant, now leading Lucknow Super Giants and fighting an uphill battle to reclaim his India spot after being sidelined post the triumphant T20 World Cup campaign in 2024.But Iyer’s race is no longer against Pant. World Cup-winning captain Suryakumar Yadav ’s prolonged slump has triggered one of the trickiest phases for Gambhir and the selection committee.Sources told TOI that Iyer is the most obvious replacement for Surya if and when selectors decide to move on. His authority at No. 4 in T20 cricket is unmatched in Indian cricket at present. The selectors showed little leniency with Pant and Shubman Gill in the format. But conviction around Surya’s successor — particularly as captain — remains key.“Iyer last played a T20I in 2023. He has not been part of the current setup. It’s a big call to bring him back and hand him the captaincy straightaway,” a BCCI source told TOI. “The focus has shifted towards the 2027 ODI World Cup, which has given selectors breathing space to plan for a T20I leadership transition.”At Punjab Kings, Iyer has been branded the sarpanch. His swagger and carefree demeanour fit the aura. It stems from self-awareness. Bowling coach James Hopes, a Ponting mainstay since their Capitals days, has witnessed a flamboyant 23-year-old evolve into a 31-year-old leader in complete command.“He’s very aware of what his perceived weaknesses were over the years. He’s come into this season having really nailed them. When he got hurt in Australia (in Oct last year), he was starting to nailing them in international cricket as well,” Hopes said here on Saturday. “Once he finds his way back into the Indian team, you’ll see the batter he’s going to be for the rest of his career. Captaincy improves his batting,” Hopes added.Iyer has always been in a comfort zone around Ponting. As a captain, there’s no need for handholding anymore. “He was very young when he was captaining Delhi. He’s very well planned, but still follows his gut,” Hopes said.“Ricky and Shreyas work well together. When we get into our meetings, he’s the captain. He steps up and he’s got his own ideas, he’s willing to listen,” he added.Iyer is already the vice-captain of the ODI team. It’s about how the Indian team management and the selectors find a place for him in the T20 team.
Article Summary: Shreyas iyer first tasted ipl captaincy in turbulent circumstances. Delhi daredevils stalwart gautam gambhir stood down owing to woeful form. Iyer went on to blossom as a captain and a batter under ricky ponting. He’s very well planned, but still follows his gut,” hopes said. Iyer is already the vice-captain of the odi team.

So far, 15 matches have been played in the new season of the IPL , and four teams have already set the early marker in the race for the playoffs.With four wins in four matches, Rajasthan Royals sit at the top of the points table. Punjab Kings are at second after two wins and a no result due to rain. They are followed by defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru, Delhi Capitals and Lucknow Super Giants, who have two wins each so far. Meanwhile, Chennai Super Kings and Kolkata Knight Riders are two winless teams in IPL 2026.With a win each, Sunrisers Hyderabad, Mumbai Indians, and Gujarat Titans have opened their account in the standings and will look to challenge the top four in coming fixtures.The ongoing IPL season has already witnessed multiple weather disruptions across the country. Article Title: Will Anderson Jr: NFL news: Will Anderson Jr signs $150 million extension with Houston Texans, becoming highest paid non QB
Article Text:
NFL news: Will Anderson Jr signs $150 million extension with Houston Texans, becoming highest paid non QB (Image via Getty)

Will Anderson Jr. earns record deal after dominant 2025 season

Will Anderson Jr. and Danielle Hunter lead Texans defense under DeMeco Ryans

NFL Network has reported a big update, and it’s hard to miss. The Houston Texans have locked in their young star Will Anderson Jr. with a three-year, $150 million extension. As per insider Ian Rapoport, the deal includes $134 million guaranteed and even a non-trade clause. That makes Anderson the highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history right now.This deal didn’t come out of nowhere. Will Anderson Jr. just had the best season of his career in 2025, and it showed in every stat. He finished with 12 sacks, which was his highest so far. On top of that, he added three forced fumbles, 54 tackles, 20 tackles for loss, and 23 quarterback hits.Then there’s pressure, something not everyone notices at first. According to Next Gen Stats, Anderson had 85 quarterback pressures, the second-most in the entire league. That tells you how often he was disrupting plays, even when he didn’t get the sack.Because of this, he earned First-Team All-Pro honors for the first time in his career. He also finished second in Defensive Player of the Year voting, just behind Myles Garrett.If you look at his growth, it’s clear. He had seven sacks as a rookie in 2023, then 11 in 2024, and now 12.Every year, he’s getting better. That steady rise made this contract feel like the next logical step.Another reason behind this deal is how well Anderson has worked with Danielle Hunter. The two have formed one of the most dangerous pass-rush duos in the league.In 2025, both players finished in the top 10 in sacks. Together, they combined for 27 sacks, which made life very hard for opposing quarterbacks. Just last month, on March 5, the Texans gave Hunter a one-year $40.1 million extension. So now, both stars are secured.Head coach DeMeco Ryans has built this team around defense. That identity is clear. And right now, Anderson is at the center of it.Since being drafted No. 3 overall in 2023, he has delivered right away. He won Defensive Rookie of the Year, made the Pro Bowl twice, and quickly became one of the most important players on the team.The Texans see him as a long-term leader. This contract proves it. They are not just rewarding past performance, they are betting on what he will keep doing in the future.
Article Summary: Will anderson jr. has signed a three-year, $150 million extension with the houston texans. The deal includes $134 million guaranteed and even a non-trade clause. It makes anderson the highest-paid non-quarterback in nfl history right now.

Article Title: Antonio Felix Da Costa: After Formula E wins, Antonio Felix da Costa set for WEC comeback at 6 Hours of Imola
Article Text:
Antonio Felix Da Costa (Image credit: Instagram)

Antonio Felix da Costa returns for 6 Hours of Imola



Antonio Felix da Costa’s Formula E performance



Antonio Felix da Costa is riding high after securing two consecutive wins in Formula E this season. The Jaguar TCS Racing driver triumphed in Jeddah before going on to win the maiden Madrid E-Prix. The 34-year-old has now confirmed his return to the World Endurance Championship, with a drive in the 6 Hours of Imola.Antonio Felix Da Costa’s return to the World Endurance Championship was confirmed via the series’ official social media channels. An Instagram post read: “Return of the DAC. After two seasons away, @afelixdacosta is back in WEC, this time with @alpine_endurance_team. Could a podium be on the cards this weekend?”Da Costa simply replied “Voilà” in the comments. He will take part in the iconic Imola race as part of the Alpine team. The event is scheduled to take place at Italy’s Autodromo Internazionale Enzo e Dino Ferrari from 17–19 April, with the race set to begin at 1 pm local time on 19 April.Da Costa last competed in the championship in 2023 with Hertz Team Jota in the Hypercar category.Antonio Felix Da Costa currently sits fourth in the Formula E Drivers’ Standings with 64 points following his back-to-back victories. He is just one point behind his team-mate Mitch Evans.After a slow start with Jaguar TCS Racing following his move from Porsche, da Costa secured his first win with the team in Round 5 in Jeddah. He followed that up with another victory in Round 6 in Madrid.Speaking after his double win, da Costa said, “Back-to-back wins, a 1–2 for the team - that doesn’t happen often! Massive congratulations to all the boys and girls back in the UK providing us with these cars. It’s kind of a home race for me. I have a lot of friends and family here and it’s a bit of a dream day. We’re both winners, we both wanted to win, we just got different messages. We’ll work it out. I’m happy for Mitch [Evans], he must have done an amazing race, I’ve got to go back and watch that because he came from the back! I’m happy with what we executed today, and it’s a dream day. ”He is using the current break in the Formula E calendar to compete in the World Endurance Championship. He will return to Formula E action for the Berlin double-header, scheduled from 1–3 May.Also read: Exclusive interview: Edoardo Mortara shines for Mahindra Racing this season, says “important to keep your feet on the ground”
Article Summary: Antonio felix da costa will drive in the 6 hours of imola. The 34-year-old last competed in the championship in 2023 with hertz team jota. He is currently fourth in the formula e drivers’ standings with 64 points.

Article Title: Steven Miller: Arizona State coach Steven Miller dies at 29 after recent medical incident at practice
Article Text:
Steven Miller (Image Via Getty)

Steven Miller remembered by Arizona State community after medical incident and sudden passing at age 29

Arizona State is dealing with heartbreaking news. Steven Miller, a 29-year-old assistant football coach, has died just days after a medical incident during practice. The university confirmed his passing on Wednesday night and quickly canceled its spring practice as the team tried to process the loss. For many around the program, the news came suddenly and has left a deep impact.Miller had been facing health issues in the days before his death. Reports say he suffered a medical episode after practice on April 7. He missed two sessions but returned to the field about a week later. While the exact cause of death has not been officially shared, it is known that he had been living with epilepsy. The situation has raised concern and sadness across the college football community.Steven Miller was not just a coach. He had been part of the Arizona State football family for years. Before stepping into coaching, he played for the Sun Devils from 2015 to 2019. He was an offensive lineman who showed steady growth during his time with the team.He played in 49 games and started 35 of them, taking roles at both tackle and guard. After redshirting in 2015, he slowly became a regular presence on the field. One of his final moments as a player came in the 2019 Sun Bowl, where Arizona State secured a win over Florida State.After his playing career ended, Miller moved into coaching. He worked as a graduate assistant at Louisiana Lafayette and later at the University of Florida. Still, Arizona State remained close to him. About three years ago, he returned to join the staff again, working under head coach Kenny Dillingham at Mountain America Stadium.Following his passing, the university shared a message on X. It read, “The Sun Devil Football family mourns the passing of Steven Miller. We extend our thoughts to those who knew and loved him.” The words reflected how much he meant to the people around him.Head coach Kenny Dillingham also shared his thoughts. He said, “Praying for everyone who knew Steve. He had a joy for people that was unmatched and impacted so many lives. We lost a good one!”Those who knew Miller often speak about his energy and the way he connected with others. Whether as a player or a coach, he brought the same passion to the field every day.Right now, the focus is on remembering him and supporting his family, friends, and teammates. His journey, though short, left a strong mark on everyone he worked with.
Article Summary: Steven miller, a 29-year-old assistant football coach at arizona state, died on wednesday. He had been facing health issues in the days before his death. Reports say he suffered a medical episode after practice on april 7. He missed two sessions but returned to the field about a week later.

Article Title: Shane Lowry Interview: Shane Lowry shares honest reason behind avoiding Rory McIlroy Masters celebration after tough Augusta finish ruined his week
Article Text:
Shane Lowry shares honest reason behind avoiding Rory McIlroy Masters celebration after tough Augusta finish ruined his week (Image via Getty)

Shane Lowry explains why he skipped Rory McIlroy celebration after Masters heartbreak at Augusta National

Rory McIlroy and Shane Lowry friendship stays strong despite Masters week ups and downs

Shane Lowry had a hard time after the Masters. Even though his close friend Rory McIlroy won big at Augusta, Lowry said he could not celebrate with him. McIlroy made history by winning back-to-back titles at Augusta National Golf Club, something last done by Tiger Woods in 2002. But for Lowry, the moment felt very different.Lowry was in a strong place after three rounds. He was fourth and still in the fight. But Sunday changed everything. He had a very tough final round and finished far down the leaderboard after shooting six over.Because of that, he said he did not feel right celebrating. Speaking to Golf Channel, as reported by the Irish Independent, Lowry shared his honest feelings.“I texted him on Sunday night, and I said, ‘Honestly, I am so happy for you, but I do not have it in me tonight to go over and celebrate.’”He added that he felt low after the round and chose to stay home instead of joining McIlroy. He said he would see him later in Florida.This is not the first time emotions ran high. Last year, Lowry was upset when reporters kept asking him about McIlroy while he was still trying to win his own round. He made it clear he wanted to focus on his game. Still, their bond is strong. They are close friends on tour and spend time together with family as well.Off the course, McIlroy and Lowry share a strong friendship. They often play together and even team up in events. In fact, they won the Zurich Classic of New Orleans in 2024 on their first try.During Masters week, they were also seen enjoying time with family at the Par-Three contest. Their wives — Erica McIlroy, Wendy Lowry, and Clare Fleetwood — walked the course together while kids ran around, showing a lighter side of the game.Lowry also created a special moment earlier in the week. He made a hole-in-one on the sixth hole. With that, he became the first player ever to record more than one ace at the Masters. His first came back in 2016.Even after the tough Sunday, Lowry said he played good golf overall but did not get the result he wanted. He returned to action at the RBC Heritage and shot one under in the opening round, still trying to find his rhythm again.Meanwhile, McIlroy skipped that event after his emotional win.
Article Summary: Shane lowry shared honest reason behind avoiding rory mcilroy masters celebration after tough augusta finish ruined his week. Lowry was in a strong place after three rounds but had a very tough final round and finished far down the leaderboard after shooting six over.

Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El rubius was eliminated from mrbeast's $1 million challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.

Article Title: Ipl Ticket Scam: IPL craze costs techie Rs 1.46 lakh in fake RCB vs CSK ticket scam
Article Text:
Image credit: AI generated pic

Watch Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy

RCB register their second win

Poll Do you think the demand for IPL tickets is too high every season? Yes, it's excessive. No, it's just right.

NEW DELHI: IPL comes and the demand for tickets and passes go soaring up by the fans. The desperation to get inside the stadium to watch their favourite teams in action in the IPL go high and high. When the tickets get sold out, the demand gets too hig, and the cricket fans even ask for tickets in black. But for a 25-year-old techie the craze for IPL tickets for RCB vs CSK turned too costly and he was duped for a massive Rs. 1.46 lakh.The techie, a resident of B Narayanapura, fell into a trap by a post posted by a fraudster who posed himself as a ticket seller on Instagram.As per a report in Deccan Herald, the techie alleged in a complaint to Mahadevapura Police that an individual claiming to be Sumit Biswal promised he could arrange IPL tickets along with food coupons.As per the techie, Biswal was introduced him as a senior supervisor for ticket counter at M Chinnaswamy Stadium"He asked me to come near the stadium gate number 10, saying someone would deliver the tickets within minutes, and even sent an email confirmation to gain my trust," the complainant told Deccan Herald."Initially, I agreed to buy two tickets for Rs 3,700 each,but he kept asking for more money under various pretexts such as refundable security deposits, additional ID cards and food coupons.Trusting him, I made multiple payments - even using my mother’s bank account after exhausting my own limits—and ended up transferring around Rs 1.46 lakh," he added.Bhuvneshwar Kumar’s incisive spell of 3-41, coming after a marauding batting show from Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) to a commanding 43-run victory over Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday.The contest saw RCB’s batting unit dismantle CSK’s attack with relentless hitting, as David’s 70 not out off just 25 balls and Patidar’s unbeaten 48 off only 19 balls provided for the late fireworks after Padikkal’s fluent 50 off 29 balls had set the tone for the daunting total.Their combined effort lifted RCB to a daunting 250/3, thus setting a new record for highest total in IPL 2026. In reply, CSK faltered under scoreboard pressure, with Sarfaraz Khan’s 50 off 24 balls and Prashant Veer’s 43 the lone acts of resistance amid a string of failures from the top order.Bhuvneshwar was at his disciplined best and even crossed the 200-mark in terms of wickets in IPL, with others also chipping in as CSK were bowled out for 207 in 19.4 overs. The comprehensive win also meant RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.
Article Summary: Royal challengers bengaluru (rcb) beat chennai super kings (csk) by 43 runs. Tim david, rajat patidar and devdutt padikkal all scored half-centuries. Rcb set a new record for highest total in ipl 2026 with 250/3.

Article Title: 80-year-old Sikh, inspired by Fauja Singh, to run London Marathon for first time
Article Text:
80-year-old Sikh, inspired by Fauja Singh, to run London Marathon for first time

Baldev Singh running

Poll Would you consider participating in a charity marathon? Yes, for a good cause Only if I trained for it No, not interested Maybe, if I had the time







An 80-year British Sikh man is running the London Marathon for the first time on April 26, inspired by Fauja Singh who encouraged him to take up walking initially.Baldev Singh Bains is doing the marathon to raise money for the Fauja Singh Clubhouse, being built in Fauja’s memory in Redbridge, on the route Fauja used to run.Fauja, the oldest marathon runner in the world, died last July after being hit by a car in his native village Beas Pind at the age of 114. He had discovered running in his 80s and ran his first marathon aged 89.Baldev knew Fauja as they lived in the same London borough, Redbridge, and went to the same gurdwara.“When Fauja improved his running, I decided I wanted to improve my health. Fauja told me to start walking, and then running, and to change my diet and think positively,” Baldev said.As recommended by Fauja, he started off by walking around the periphery of Beal High School Redbridge.“At that time I was on medication for diabetes and other conditions and overweight. Now I have lost 30kg and my doctor has now taken me off virtually all my medication.”Baldev hired Fauja’s coach, Harmander Singh, last year to train him, and he is also a member of the Sikhs in the City running club, where Fauja trained. Harmander (66) is also running the London Marathon to raise funds for the clubhouse, which will cost £1 million. It was given planning permission on April 1, which would have been Fauja’s 115th birthday.Baldev was born in Thana, a village in Garhshankar Tehsil in Hoshiarpur district, Punjab. He read engineering at Delhi University before moving to the UK. “The way Fauja helped me, I hope this will inspire others,” Baldev said.
Article Summary: 80-year-old sikh, inspired by fauja singh, to run london marathon for first time. Baldev singh bains is doing the marathon to raise money for the fauja singh clubhouse, being built in his memory in redbridge.fauja, the oldest marathon runner in the world, died last july.

Article Title: IShowSpeed steals WrestleMania spotlight by putting Logan Paul through table after hitting Cristiano Ronaldo celebration
Article Text:
Logan Paul and IShowSpeed (Image Via Getty)

WWE

broadcast footage and live audience reactions

IShowSpeed and Logan Paul create viral WrestleMania moment with table spot and Cristiano Ronaldo celebration

Dexerto on X







Something unexpected happened at WrestleMania this year, and it had nothing to do with a planned storyline. Popular streamer IShowSpeed suddenly stepped into the ring and became part of the action. In a moment that quickly spread online, he jumped from the top rope and slammed Logan Paul through a table. The crowd inside the arena reacted instantly, and the clip started gaining attention across social media within minutes.What made the moment even more interesting was what happened just before the move. Speed paused, looked around, and performed Cristiano Ronaldo’s famous “Siuuu” celebration. That small detail turned a shocking wrestling spot into something fans from both wrestling and football could connect with. As shared by, the entire sequence felt more like a crossover moment than a regular match interruption.The moment happened during one of the ongoing matches at WrestleMania, which is known as WWE’s biggest show of the year. While the focus is usually on professional wrestlers, this time the spotlight shifted quickly. Speed ran into the ring without much warning, climbed up, and delivered the move that sent Logan Paul crashing through a table placed inside the ring.Logan Paul, who has become a regular part of WWE shows, is known for mixing entertainment with athletic moments.But this time, he found himself on the receiving end of a viral highlight. The timing, the jump, and the table impact all came together in a way that felt intense but also very real to the audience watching live.Right before the jump, Speed made sure to do something that his followers instantly recognized. He performed Cristiano Ronaldo’s “Siuuu” celebration with full energy. It was quick, but it added personality to the moment. Fans in the arena reacted loudly, and many could be heard cheering even before he leaped.As clips started circulating online, the moment gained traction fast. Platforms like X and Instagram saw the video spread widely, with users pointing out how unusual it was to see a streamer take part in such a major wrestling event. According to clips shared by, the reaction online matched the energy inside the stadium.This crossover between streaming and wrestling shows how entertainment is changing. Moments like these are not just about sports anymore. They mix internet culture, live events, and global icons into one scene that people remember.
Article Summary: Streamer ishowspeed jumped from the top rope and slammed logan paul through a table. The crowd inside the arena reacted instantly, and the clip started gaining attention across social media within minutes. Speed paused, looked around, and performed cristiano ronaldo’s famous “siuuu’ celebration.
